title: How This Investor Helped After a Nuclear Disaster
summary: Jesse Michels interviews Josh Wolfe, co-founder and managing director of Lux Capital, discussing his background growing up in Brooklyn, his investment philosophy focused on hard science and frontier technology, and notable portfolio companies including Curion (nuclear waste cleanup), Variant Bio (genetic research in remote populations), and space-related ventures. The conversation covers nuclear energy, genetic technologies, the future of space exploration, and New York City's tech ecosystem.

acronyms.definition: U.S. government department responsible for energy policy and nuclear programs, including nuclear waste cleanup
Research approach to identify genetic variants associated with particular traits or diseases

events.description: Lux Capital co-founded Curion, a nuclear waste cleanup company
Fukushima nuclear disaster occurred, leading to Curion's involvement in cleanup efforts

key_claims.claim: 25% of the Department of Energy's budget is spent on nuclear waste cleanup
Curion's revenue grew from $1 million to nearly $200 million in just a few years after Fukushima
Curion was able to remove 99% of the radiation from Fukushima
Most genetic sequencing has been done on white Europeans because that's where the funding is
There are $152 billion locked into venture capital currently
Russia launched the first movie to be filmed in space (not a documentary)
Jeff Bezos raised $300 million in an IPO and never raised another dollar of capital
Russia declined to partner with the US on a lunar base and chose to work with China instead
